title: Layout Managers, Resize, Scaling, High DPI, and Reflow · Language Clinic: Responsive Cell Size
summary: The Canvas has fixed pixels, controls vanish on a small window, pack and grid conflict in one parent, and high-DPI text clips. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the responsive cell size increment in responsive desktop layout and resize policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.
Use one geometry manager per parent and configure grid weights, sticky behavior, minimum sizes, and padding intentionally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_layout_resize_scaling_3(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-responsive-cell-size-1987ei
# Competency: maze-responsive-cell-size.
# Case 5600: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_responsive_cell_size_1987ei(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1987ei = "1987ei-ie7891-5600"
    assert evidence_variant_1987ei
    evidence_axes_1987ei = (
        "grid1987ei",
        "topology5600",
        "searchie7891",
        "event1987ei5600",
        "access56001987ei",
        "recoveryie78915600",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1987ei)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Responsive Cell Size model · Theory Responsive Cell Si…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Responsive Cell Size precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Responsive Cell Size model · Theory Responsive Cell Si…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3388 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; the prior good state must survive failure; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates.
